other_observers: seen on the first occasion with Cole Wild. Seen on the second with Cole Wild, Christian Nunes, Joey Kellner, and Steve Stachowiak; first found by Glenn Walbek
description: the bird was a large gull, larger than any other gull out on the reservoir. It was slightly larger than nearby Herring Gulls. The overall shape was bulkier than that of Herring Gull, with a more chesty look, thicker neck, shorter and wider wings, and larger bill adding to the perception of bulkiness. The bill was large and thick, for a gull, with a strong gonydeal angle giving it a "blob-tipped" look. It was entirely black. The legs were medium pink in color, and relatively shorter than those of Herring Gull. The bird was too far away to observe the color of the eye.  The plumage of the bird was evenly colored all over the body, and was a light tan colored, significantly paler than the brown of juvenile Herring Gulls. The only parts of the bird that were darker was the tail, which was a couple of shades darker, but still significantly paler than that of Herring Gull, and a very slightly darker "helmet" on the head.Behaviors: The bird was observed out on the middle of Antero Reservoir. During the first observation it was observed flying around over the water and then landing on the far shore next to a California Gull. During the second observation it was observed going from water hole to water hole on the mostly frozen lake, usually in the company of a few Herring Gulls.Call: none heardPlumage: first basic
